key_metrics: Inventories: $7.73 billion (up from $7.49 billion at May 31, 2025)
qoq_changes: Cash and equivalents decreased to $6.97 billion from $7.46 billion at the end of the prior fiscal year.
Inventories increased to $7.73 billion from $7.49 billion at the end of the prior fiscal year.
new_risks: Margin compression is evident as gross profit declined 6.4% despite flat revenue.
Rising marketing costs to stimulate sales, shown by a 13.5% increase in demand creation expense.
Qualitative risk factors (Item 1A) were omitted from the provided excerpt, but financial risks point to profitability pressures.
management_tone: Cautious; while top-line revenue remained stable, significant margin compression and a 32% drop in net income indicate profitability headwinds and increased costs to drive demand.
analyst_note: Nike's quarter ended November 30, 2025, shows stagnant revenue growth (+0.6% YoY) coupled with a sharp 31.9% decline in net income. The combination of lower gross margins, rising inventory levels, and a 13.5% spike in demand creation expenses suggests the company is facing a highly promotional environment and elevated costs to maintain market share.
